data.title: UTulsa and Hurricane Ventures invest in BioReact, an AI platform for bioprocess dev. Funding to accelerate growth.
data.description: In a recent announcement on February 12, 2026, in Tulsa, Oklahoma, the University of Tulsa and Hurricane Ventures disclosed their investment in BioReact. This platform utilizes artificial intelligence and analytics to enhance the efficiency of bioprocess development. The investment aims to expedite the growth of BioReact and further advance its capabilities in the industry.

data.title: Coinbase reports $667M loss in Q4 as crypto markets decline, missing Wall Street estimates.
data.description: In the fourth quarter, Coinbase reported its first net loss since the third quarter of 2023, falling short of Wall Street expectations for earnings. This marks a significant departure from previous quarters and reflects a challenging period for the crypto exchange. The specific figures of the loss were not disclosed in the news.

data.title: Ninety-three Greenberg Traurig attorneys and 29 practice areas recognized in 2026 Chambers Global Guide.
data.description: Ninety-three lawyers from Greenberg Traurig, LLP, a leading global law firm, have been acknowledged in the 2026 Chambers Global Guide. This esteemed legal services directory also lauded the firm for excellence in 29 practice areas spanning 13 regions worldwide.

data.title: Neptune Insurance Holdings to reveal Q4 2025 financial results on Feb 18, 2026, after market closes. Conference call at 5:00 PM ET.
data.description: Neptune Insurance Holdings Inc. will release its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on February 18, 2026, after the market closes. The parent company of Neptune Flood Incorporated, listed as NP on NYSE, will discuss the results in a conference call and webcast at 5:00 PM ET on the same day. The dial-in number for the call will be provided by the company.

data.title: LA opens spring 2026 youth sports registration, providing affordable access for kids aged 3-17 citywide.
data.description: The City of Los Angeles Department of Recreation and Parks is introducing the PlayLA Youth & Adaptive Youth Sports Program this spring. Families in Los Angeles are encouraged to participate, with registration currently available for spring leagues, classes, and clinics. These programs provide accessible and affordable sports and recreation opportunities at various locations including recreation centers, aquatic facilities, and partner sites throughout the city. The initiative is inclusive, catering to youth aged 3–17. Parents and caregivers can sign up their children now.

data.title: Stock market falls, S&P 500 below 50-day line. AI disruption trade intensifies; Applied Materials, Arista Networks report strong earnings.
data.description: On Thursday, the stock market experienced a significant decline, resulting in the S&P 500 falling below its 50-day moving average. The trade related to artificial intelligence (AI) disruption is gaining momentum. Following their earnings reports released late, Applied Materials and Arista Networks saw a notable surge. The market sentiment is being influenced by concerns regarding AI, as reflected in the Dow Jones Futures. These companies experienced a late boost in their stock prices post-earnings reports.

data.title: Dow plunges in market sell-off, S&P 500 falls for 2026. Cisco, AppLovin stocks plummet on earnings news.
data.description: Today, the stock market experienced a significant decline with the Dow Jones index dropping during a market sell-off on Thursday. Tech companies AppLovin and Cisco also took a hit on their earnings, leading to a sharp dive in their stock prices. The S&P 500 turned negative for the year 2026 amidst this downturn. This information was part of live coverage on the market's performance.

data.title: Corsair Gaming's Q4 revenue up 6% to $436.86 million; EPS beats at 43 cents vs 27 cents estimate, driving stock surge.
data.description: Corsair Gaming (NASDAQ: CRSR) released its Q4 financial results post-market close on Thursday, with impressive figures driving shares higher. In the fourth quarter, the company reported a revenue of $436.86 million, marking a 6% increase from the previous year and surpassing the consensus estimate of $421.99 million. The earnings per share for Q4 were reported at 43 cents, exceeding the Street's estimated 27 cents per share. Particularly, the Gaming Components and Systems segment saw a revenue growth of 11% year-over-year in Q4, attributed to a strong demand for memory products. Despite challenges in the global semiconductor market, Corsair managed to secure supply to meet the high global demand. For more details, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Chorus Aviation sees strong financial growth in Q4 2025: Net income of $16.7M, 38% dividend increase to $0.11/CS, $100M in share buybacks planned over 4 years.
data.description: Chorus Aviation Inc. reported a significant increase in its fourth quarter 2025 net income from continuing operations to $16.7 million, a quarter-over-quarter increase of $66.1 million. Annual net income also saw a substantial increase from $15.8 million in 2024 to $78.7 million in 2025. The company purchased and cancelled 3,759,929 Common Shares, announced a 38% increase in quarterly dividends to $0.11 per Common Share, and plans to buy back up to $100 million in Common Shares over four years. Additionally, Chorus aims to acquire Kadex Aero Supply for approximately $50 million. This marks a strategic move to accelerate growth and enhance shareholder returns. The company's Adjusted Earnings available to Common Shareholders showed significant improvement, with a year-over-year increase from $0.97 per Common Share in 2024 to $2.27 in 2025. Chorus also reported a full year Free Cash Flow of $135.3 million, a year-over-year increase of $16.5 million. The consolidated financial analysis from continuing operations showed positive growth across various financial metrics, indicating a strong financial performance by Chorus Aviation Inc.

data.title: Toast Inc's stock drops post Q4 earnings report. Q4 revenue: $1.63B. Q4 EPS: 16 cents. ARR grew 26% over $2B. GPV up 22% to $51.4B.
data.description: Toast Inc (NYSE: TOST) saw a decrease in after-hours trading following the release of their Q4 financial results. Q4 revenue came in at $1.63 billion, exceeding the estimated $1.62 billion, with Q4 EPS at 16 cents compared to the projected 13 cents. The Annualized Recurring Run-Rate (ARR) rose by 26% to over $2 billion by Dec. 31, 2025, while Gross Payment Volume (GPV) saw a 22% year-over-year increase to $51.4 billion. Toast remains optimistic despite stock testing lower boundaries, offering more details on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Wilks Development announces TYLER'S as first retail tenant at Firefly Park, leasing 16,653 sq ft for 18-story tower opening Fall 2027 in Frisco, Texas.
data.description: A Texas sportswear retailer, TYLER'S, has committed to anchor an 18-story residential tower at Aurora, Firefly Park in Frisco. The tower is slated to open in Fall 2027. Wilks Development revealed that TYLER'S has signed a lease for 16,653 square feet in the development.

data.title: Prologis Board approves 6% increase in quarterly dividend to $4.28 per share.
data.description: Today in San Francisco, Prologis, Inc. (NYSE: PLD) announced a 6% increase in its annualized dividend to $4.28 per share of common stock. The Board of Directors approved this decision, declaring a regular cash dividend for the quarter ending March 31, 2026.

data.title: Marriott International declares quarterly cash dividend of 67 cents per share, payable on March 31, 2026.
data.description: Bethesda-based Marriott International, Inc. (Nasdaq: MAR) has disclosed a quarterly cash dividend of 67 cents per common stock share. This dividend will be paid out on March 31, 2026, to shareholders registered as of the record date.

data.title: Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE: ACHR) to report Q4 & full year 2025 results on March 2, 2026. Webcast at 2:00 p.m. PT. Call +1 646-844-6383 or +1 833-470-1428.
data.description: In Santa Clara, California, Archer Aviation Inc. (NYSE: ACHR) is set to disclose its operating update and financial results for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025 after the market closes on Monday, March 2, 2026. A live webcast discussing these results will be hosted at 2 p.m. Pacific Time. The webcast and its replay can be accessed at investors.archer.com. For participation in the conference call, individuals can dial +1 646-844-6383 (domestic) or +1 833-470-1428 (international), and input the access code 043388.

data.title: Lambda appoints Jerry Hunter as Vice Chairman and Special Advisor, guiding long-term infrastructure strategy. Hunter has over a decade at Sun Microsystems and helped build AWS's global data center operations.
data.description: In San Francisco, Lambda, the Superintelligence Cloud, has appointed Jerry Hunter as Vice Chairman, Compute Delivery and Special Advisor to the Board. Hunter will oversee Lambda’s infrastructure strategy and bolster the company's initiatives in deploying and managing large-scale AI factories. With experience spanning over ten years at Sun Microsystems in the early days of networked computing and his contributions to Amazon in constructing AWS's worldwide data center operations, Hunter brings valuable expertise to Lambda's team.

data.title: US spent billions on drone defense tech, but gaps remain. Recent El Paso incident underscores need for further testing.
data.description: Billions of dollars have been invested by the United States in the development of counter-drone technology, with a significant portion awaiting further real-world testing.

data.title: cbdMD, Inc. (NYSE: YCBD) to discuss Q1 results on Feb. 17, 2026 at 4:20 p.m. Eastern Time.532 characters.
data.description: In Charlotte, North Carolina on February 12, 2026, cbdMD, Inc. (NYSE American: YCBD), a prominent CBD company, will conduct a conference call at 4:20 p.m. EST on Tuesday, February 17, 2026. The call aims to discuss the company's recent developments and future plans.

data.title: Dutch Bros Inc's Q4 revenue up 29.4% to $443.61 million, beats Street estimate; EPS at 17 cents, beats 10 cents prediction.
data.description: Dutch Bros Inc (NYSE: BROS) released its Q4 financial report after market close, revealing revenue of $443.61 million, marking a 29.4% increase from the previous year and surpassing the estimated $424.75 million. Earnings per share stood at 17 cents, exceeding the projected 10 cents per share. They also reported a 7.7% growth in same-store sales for the quarter, emphasizing their 19-year streak of positive same-store sales growth. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Advantage Energy Ltd. had $287.7 million in net capital expenditures; 2025 production reached 79,823 boe/d, with production outlook of 80,000 boe/d in January 2026. The company hedged 33% of forecasted gas production for 2026 & 11% for 2027, and approximately 27% of oil and condensate for the same periods. Reserves: Proved Developed Producing reserves increased by 1%, with PDP reserves at $1.4 billion, 1P reserves at $2.8 billion, and 2P reserves at $4.1 billion. Advantage is focusing on operational excellence, financial discipline, and shareholder returns. The Board concluded a strategic review which determined continued execution of the strategic plan as the best approach for shareholder value. 2026 capital reduced by $20 million, 2026 production guidance unchanged despite reducing capital.
data.description: Advantage Energy Ltd. provided a corporate update and announced its 2025 reserves. The company had net capital expenditures of $287.7 million in 2025, with production averaging 79,823 boe/d in Q4, increasing to 80,000 boe/d in January 2026. They divested a non-producing asset for $12 million and increased gas hedging to 33% for 2026. The company concluded its strategic review, deciding to focus on its strategic plan for shareholder value. In 2026, they plan to reduce capital by $20 million while maintaining production growth. The 2025 reserves saw increases in PDP, 1P, and 2P reserves, with F&D costs of $9.36/boe for PDP. The company's net present value of reserves was $4.1 billion for 2P reserves. The company's strategy focuses on operational excellence, financial discipline, and maximizing shareholder returns. They also plan to reduce debt and potentially initiate share buybacks. Moving forward, they plan to focus on Glacier operations, maintaining production growth with cost efficiency.
